content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     :   File                                                Date    :   Jan 3, 1979




From   :   FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
           Barbara Campbell

Subject:   Lobbyist Employer Authorization

     In response to a question from Lance Olson, I advised
that it was acceptable for a lobbyist employer to execute
one form authorizing several lobbyists and to file that
form and copies with the Secretary of State's office as
long as there were a sufficient number of copies to meet
the filing needs of the Secretary of State's office.

     Although authorizations have been filed in this manner
last year, the Secretary of State's office questioned
such a filing this year.

BC:plh

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a lobbyist employer may execute a single authorization form covering multiple lobbyists and file copies with the Secretary of State's office.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: It is acceptable for a lobbyist employer to execute one authorization form for multiple lobbyists and file that form with sufficient copies to the Secretary of State's office, though such filings have been questioned by the Secretary of State despite prior acceptance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal memorandum format rather than structured advice letter; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; appears to be an internal memo documenting advice given to Lance Olson; lacks traditional letter structure

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a lobbyist employer may execute a single authorization form covering multiple lobbyists and file copies with the Secretary of State's office.

CONCLUSION: It is acceptable for a lobbyist employer to execute one authorization form for multiple lobbyists and file that form with sufficient copies to the Secretary of State's office, though such filings have been questioned by the Secretary of State despite prior acceptance.

Although authorizations have been filed in this manner last year, the Secretary of State's office questioned such a filing this year. BC:plh
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum from Barbara Campbell advising that a lobbyist employer may execute a single authorization form covering multiple lobbyists and file it with the Secretary of State, though this practice has raised questions despite being done previously.
